verses.text: At the thunder Elihu's heart pounds; God 'thundereth marvellously with his voice' and does great things beyond our understanding. The storm fills him with awe. The works of God should move us to wonder, not to argument.

At the thunder Elihu's heart pounds; God 'thundereth marvellously with his voice' and does great things beyond our understanding. The storm fills him with awe. The works of God should move us to wonder, not to argument.

At the thunder Elihu's heart pounds; God 'thundereth marvellously with his voice' and does great things beyond our understanding. The storm fills him with awe. The works of God should move us to wonder, not to argument.

At the thunder Elihu's heart pounds; God 'thundereth marvellously with his voice' and does great things beyond our understanding. The storm fills him with awe. The works of God should move us to wonder, not to argument.

God tells the snow and rain to fall, sealing up man's work so that all may know His hand, while the beasts take cover. Winter is His command. He can halt our labours with a word, to teach us our dependence.

God tells the snow and rain to fall, sealing up man's work so that all may know His hand, while the beasts take cover. Winter is His command. He can halt our labours with a word, to teach us our dependence.

God tells the snow and rain to fall, sealing up man's work so that all may know His hand, while the beasts take cover. Winter is His command. He can halt our labours with a word, to teach us our dependence.
The whirlwind comes from the south and frost by the breath of God, freezing the broad waters. The seasons obey Him. Cold and storm alike are servants doing the bidding of their Maker.
The whirlwind comes from the south and frost by the breath of God, freezing the broad waters. The seasons obey Him. Cold and storm alike are servants doing the bidding of their Maker.

He loads the clouds and turns them by His counsel, whether for correction, for His land, or for mercy. Every cloud serves His purpose. The same rain that judges one place blesses another, all by His wise design.

He loads the clouds and turns them by His counsel, whether for correction, for His land, or for mercy. Every cloud serves His purpose. The same rain that judges one place blesses another, all by His wise design.

He loads the clouds and turns them by His counsel, whether for correction, for His land, or for mercy. Every cloud serves His purpose. The same rain that judges one place blesses another, all by His wise design.

'Stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God,' Elihu urges Job. He calls for quiet contemplation. Often the cure for a troubled, questioning heart is simply to stop and behold the greatness of God.

'Stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God,' Elihu urges Job. He calls for quiet contemplation. Often the cure for a troubled, questioning heart is simply to stop and behold the greatness of God.

'Stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God,' Elihu urges Job. He calls for quiet contemplation. Often the cure for a troubled, questioning heart is simply to stop and behold the greatness of God.

Can Job, who only feels the heat in his garments, spread out the skies firm as a molten mirror? He cannot match the least of God's works. Our helplessness before creation should silence our complaints against the Creator.

Can Job, who only feels the heat in his garments, spread out the skies firm as a molten mirror? He cannot match the least of God's works. Our helplessness before creation should silence our complaints against the Creator.

Elihu confesses they cannot even order their speech before such a God; would any dare demand to address Him? Awe stops his mouth. Before infinite majesty, the wise learn to speak little and reverently.

Elihu confesses they cannot even order their speech before such a God; would any dare demand to address Him? Awe stops his mouth. Before infinite majesty, the wise learn to speak little and reverently.

Men cannot look at the sun when the wind has cleared the sky; how much less on God, with whom is 'terrible majesty.' His glory dazzles beyond bearing. If we cannot face the sun He made, how shall we face Him?

Men cannot look at the sun when the wind has cleared the sky; how much less on God, with whom is 'terrible majesty.' His glory dazzles beyond bearing. If we cannot face the sun He made, how shall we face Him?

The Almighty we cannot fully find out, excellent in power and justice, yet not afflicting without cause; therefore men should fear Him. He is just as well as great. The fitting end of all such thoughts is humble reverence.

The Almighty we cannot fully find out, excellent in power and justice, yet not afflicting without cause; therefore men should fear Him. He is just as well as great. The fitting end of all such thoughts is humble reverence.
